reasoning: CompaniesMarketCap explicitly categorizes all five of the current top-valued companies (NVIDIA, Apple, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon) under its "Tech" sector. As of April 2025, the 5th largest company (Amazon) has a market cap of approximately $2.56 trillion. The largest non-tech company, Saudi Aramco, was most recently valued at around $1.78 trillion. This creates a massive ~$750 billion buffer (about 30% of Amazon's market cap) between the 5th place tech company and the nearest non-tech challenger. While tech stocks are volatile and a 30% correction over the next 8 months is possible (which could allow Aramco to slip into the top 5 if oil prices remain steady or rise), the sheer size of the gap makes this scenario unlikely. Giving a slight chance to a targeted tech crash or an oil surge, a 91% probability reflects the very high likelihood that tech will maintain a clean sweep of the top 5 spots through December 31, 2025.
